steps.title: Rearrange and simplify by dividing by 20
steps.content:
Starting with $\frac{20}{p}=\frac{20}{q}-\frac{20}{r}-\frac{20}{s}$, rearrange the terms to isolate the fraction containing $q$:

$$\frac{20}{q} = \frac{20}{p} + \frac{20}{r} + \frac{20}{s}$$

Divide both sides by 20:

$$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{r} + \frac{1}{s}$$

steps.title: Combine fractions with common denominator
steps.content:
To add the fractions on the right, express each with common denominator $prs$:

$$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{rs}{prs} + \frac{ps}{prs} + \frac{pr}{prs} = \frac{rs + ps + pr}{prs}$$

steps.title: Invert both sides to solve for q
steps.content:
Take the reciprocal of both sides:

$$q = \frac{prs}{pr + ps + rs}$$

This matches choice C — C is correct.
